evals.prompt:
We're mid-review on the fictional Harborline Freight engagement. Engagement root is engagements/harborline/, the built plugin is at engagements/harborline/plugins/harborline-ops/ with six built skills: quote-builder, rate-lookup, rate-audit, claims-intake, claims-followup, carrier-onboarding. The champion just exported her notes from the dashboard:

[catalog]
- merge claims-intake and claims-followup into one skill called claims
- rename quote-builder to quoting

[skill] rate-audit
- the variance tolerance is 2%, not 5% — fix it everywhere it appears

[skill] quoting
- outputs should be more standard

[eval] rate-lookup#2
- this expectation references the FreightRate API; we retired that, we're on RateHub now

And in chat she added: 'also purge every mention of FreightRate anywhere in the plugin, not just that eval.'

Normalize her notes and lay out exactly what will change — buckets, catalog operations, and the revision-workflow inputs — before running anything.
evals.expected_output: A note-normalization report that assigns every note to exactly one bucket, plans the merge and rename across both catalog.json and the plugin tree, routes the FreightRate purge as a global rule with a grep sweep, and asks the champion one clarifying question about the ambiguous 'more standard' note instead of guessing.
expectations: Every exported note is assigned to exactly one bucket (catalog change, per-skill note, or global rule), and the [eval] rate-lookup#2 note lands as a per-skill note on rate-lookup — not as a catalog change or a direct hand-edit of the eval file

The ambiguous 'outputs should be more standard' note on quoting produces a clarifying question back to the champion; it is neither silently dropped nor turned into an invented change queued for the revision run

The merge and rename plan covers both catalog.json and the plugin tree — it names the directory moves, the frontmatter name updates, and a check of composes references and router commands, not just a catalog.json edit
The FreightRate purge is captured as a global rule with a post-workflow grep sweep over the whole plugin, rather than being narrowed to the rate-lookup eval that first mentioned it

evals.prompt:
Fictional Tessellate Insurance engagement, review round two. The revise-skills workflow just finished for the plugin at engagements/tessellate/plugins/tessellate-claims/. Run record:

{
  "revised": 2, "cleanFirstTry": 2, "forcedFixes": [], "unresolved": [], "lost": [],
  "descriptionsChanged": ["policy-lookup"],
  "regressionCases": [{ "source": "review note", "finding": "claims-triage guessed the policy on a water-damage claim with no policy number instead of routing to the manual-match queue", "guardedBy": "claims-triage · trigger-evals.json", "status": "open" }]
}

This round's global rule was: purge every mention of QuoteMaster. A grep of the plugin tree after the workflow shows two surviving hits: one inside a should_trigger:false case in skills/claims-triage/evals/trigger-evals.json, and one in step 3 of skills/policy-lookup/SKILL.md. Close out the review round and report.
evals.expected_output: A close-out that appends the regression case to .build/regressions.json as an open review-note regression, re-runs the trigger benchmark because policy-lookup's description changed, fixes the SKILL.md QuoteMaster survivor while keeping the negative-eval occurrence with a written justification, regenerates and republishes the dashboard at its stable path, and reports changes, re-verify results, and push-backs.
expectations: The run record's regressionCases entries are appended verbatim to .build/regressions.json (they arrive ledger-shaped: source, finding, guardedBy, status "open"); leaving them only in the run record, reshaping them, or recording them as already passing, fails

Because descriptionsChanged is non-empty, the trigger benchmark re-run is executed (or explicitly issued as the required next action) before the round is reported done — a close-out that declares done with the stale trigger score fails
The two QuoteMaster survivors get opposite treatment: the should_trigger:false eval occurrence is kept with a written justification, and the policy-lookup SKILL.md step-3 occurrence is fixed

The dashboard is regenerated via gen-dashboard.py and republished to the same file path so the champion's link stays stable, and the final report separately states what changed, what passed re-verify, and any notes pushed back on with reasons
